result.markdown:
--- title: "Endangered Languages of the World" subtitle: "INFO 526 - Summer 2026 - Final Project" author: "Damon Kelson" title-slide-attributes: data-background-image: images/free_german_text.jpg data-background-size: stretch data-background-opacity: "0.2" data-slide-number: none format: revealjs: theme: ['data/customtheming.scss'] editor: visual execute: echo: false --- ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell} ::: ## Dataset & Analysis Questions
* Dataset: Glottolog 5.2.1 (TidyTuesday) * ~8,000 documented languages. * Geographic coordinates, language families, endangerment status, isolate status... * Analysis Questions: * How does geography correlate with endangered language status? * Are language isolates and small language families more vulnerable?
## Endangerment Risk by Geographic Macroarea ::: {.fill} :::: {.columns} ::: {.column width="70%"} ::: {.cell} ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](presentation_files/figure-revealjs/macroarea plot-1.png){fig-align='center' width=100%} ::: ::: ::: ::: {.column width="30%"}
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Region % at risk Number of Languages
Australia 99% 134
South America 91% 382
North America 87% 500
Papunesia 65% 2041
Eurasia 64% 1623
Africa 40% 2145
````` ::: ::: ::: :::: ::: ## Regional Patterns of Language Endangerment ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](presentation_files/figure-revealjs/hex plot-1.png){fig-align='center' width=100%} ::: ::: ## Language Endangerment
by Family Size ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](presentation_files/figure-revealjs/bar chart slide-1.png){fig-align='center' width=100%} ::: ::: ## 66% of Language Isolates are
Concentrated in Two Regions ::: {.fill} ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](presentation_files/figure-revealjs/isolate regions side by side-1.png){width=100%} ::: ::: ::: ## Conclusions & Limitations
* Highest proportions of endangered languages occur across the Americas and Papunesia. * Only 3% of isolates are not endangered, 44% already extinct However... * Geographic coordinates are approximate, not unimpeachably robust * Endangerment status categories hide deeper nuances * Complex web of causative factors, no simple narrative can adequately describe all scenarios * Limited data: no extinction data approximations, "unclear" groups separated early in analysis

include-after-body:
<script>
  // htmlwidgets need to know to resize themselves when slides are shown/hidden.
  // Fire the "slideenter" event (handled by htmlwidgets.js) when the current
  // slide changes (different for each slide format).
  (function () {
    // dispatch for htmlwidgets
    function fireSlideEnter() {
      const event = window.document.createEvent("Event");
      event.initEvent("slideenter", true, true);
      window.document.dispatchEvent(event);
    }

    function fireSlideChanged(previousSlide, currentSlide) {
      fireSlideEnter();

      // dispatch for shiny
      if (window.jQuery) {
        if (previousSlide) {
          window.jQuery(previousSlide).trigger("hidden");
        }
        if (currentSlide) {
          window.jQuery(currentSlide).trigger("shown");
        }
      }
    }

    // hookup for slidy
    if (window.w3c_slidy) {
      window.w3c_slidy.add_observer(function (slide_num) {
        // slide_num starts at position 1
        fireSlideChanged(null, w3c_slidy.slides[slide_num - 1]);
      });
    }

  })();
</script>
